title: Vibe-driven music generation via ElevenLabs Music API
description:
Add a /music command and MCP tool that generates short ambient tracks based on the current session vibe. Uses the ElevenLabs Music API (elevenlabs.music.compose) to synthesize music from a text prompt derived from vibe_tags and vibe mood. The generated track plays via the existing playback queue.

Key design points:
- New MCP tool: music(prompt, duration_ms, ephemeral) — generate and play a short track
- New /music command: /music [prompt] — generate from explicit prompt, or derive from current vibe if no prompt given
- Vibe-to-prompt mapping: translate vibe_tags like [warm] [satisfied] into music prompt descriptions (genre, tempo, mood)
- Default duration: short ambient clips (10-30s) suitable for background/transition moments
- ElevenLabs Music API: elevenlabs.music.compose(prompt=..., music_length_ms=...) returns streaming MP3 chunks
- Requires ElevenLabs paid plan (same API key as TTS)
- Output: ephemeral by default (.tts/ dir), or saved to output dir
- Playback via existing flock-serialized enqueue()

Multi-session safety:
- New config field: music: y|n (default n) in .tts/config.md
- New /music on|off command to toggle
- Only one session can play music at a time — music is sustained audio, not short chimes
- Use a system-wide flock (e.g. ~/.punt-tts/music.lock) to ensure exclusive playback
- If another session holds the lock, skip music silently (log debug)
- Chimes and speech continue to work normally across sessions (short, serialized)
- /music on in one session implicitly means other sessions won't play music until it releases

Reference: https://elevenlabs.io/docs/eleven-api/guides/cookbooks/music/quickstart

title: Notification state management: extend tts.local.md with notify/speak fields
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 1: Rename Python package punt_tts → punt_vox
description:
Core mechanical rename of the Python package.

- Rename src/punt_tts/ directory → src/punt_vox/
- Update all Python imports (from punt_tts → from punt_vox)
- Update pyproject.toml: package name, entry points (tts → vox, tts-server → vox-server)
- Update __init__.py package metadata
- Update all test imports and references
- Run quality gates to confirm nothing broke

This is the largest single phase (~200 occurrences across 22 files) but purely mechanical.
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-session voice selection via /voice
description: When running multiple Claude Code sessions with TTS, each session should be able to set its own voice so the user can distinguish them audibly. /voice should support selecting a voice name (e.g. /voice matilda, /voice aria). Voice selection needs to persist for the session and be passed through to all speak tool calls. Consider: storing in tts.local.md, passing as a default to the MCP server, voice list/preview commands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Screen reader mode: vocalize any plugin tool output
description:
Screen reader mode for Claude Code sessions. When voice is enabled, a PostToolUse hook vocalizes MCP tool output from any plugin — not just tts tools. Acts like a screen reader: speaks whatever text appears in tool results using the session default voice.

Mechanism: PostToolUse hook matches MCP tool results (configurable allowlist/blocklist). Extracts text content and instructs Claude to call speak with ephemeral=true.

Scope: Generic — no per-tool voices, no creative pacing. Uses session default voice for everything. Biff tools excluded from allowlist when biff voice mode is active (avoids double-speaking).

Complementary to tts-51v (continuous notify). Screen reader fires synchronously after each tool call. Continuous mode fires asynchronously during long operations when the model is busy and hooks can't fire.

Config: allowlist/blocklist in .tts/config.md to control which tools get vocalized.

title: /speak command: toggle voice vs chime y|n
close_reason: Closed

title: install.sh: handle SSH auth failure gracefully
description: GitHub issue #8. install.sh runs 'claude plugin install tts@punt-labs' which clones via SSH. Fails for HTTPS-only users (no SSH keys). Fix: detect SSH connectivity, fall back to HTTPS git rewrite, clean up after. Affects any user without GitHub SSH keys configured (common on fresh Linux installs). See https://github.com/punt-labs/tts/issues/8 for suggested fix.
close_reason: PR #9 merged — SSH probe + HTTPS fallback in install.sh

title: Per-signal chime assets for continuous mode
description:
chime_done.mp3 is overloaded — it plays for task completion AND all 6 watcher signals (tests-pass, tests-fail, lint-pass, lint-fail, git-push-ok, merge-conflict). You can't distinguish success from failure by sound alone.

Step 1: Create distinct chime assets for each signal type:
- chime_done.mp3 — task completion (existing, keep as-is)
- chime_prompt.mp3 — permission/idle prompt (existing, keep as-is)
- chime_tests_pass.mp3 — bright, ascending
- chime_tests_fail.mp3 — low, descending
- chime_lint_pass.mp3 — light, crisp
- chime_lint_fail.mp3 — dull thud
- chime_git_push_ok.mp3 — triumphant, resolved
- chime_merge_conflict.mp3 — dissonant, alert

Step 2: Wire signal-to-chime mapping:
- watcher.py: _announce_chime() takes signal name, resolves to correct asset
- resolve_chime_path() becomes resolve_chime_path(signal) with per-signal lookup
- Fallback: if a signal-specific chime is missing, play chime_done.mp3

Asset generation options:
- Generate via ElevenLabs Music API (short 1-2s clips)
- Source from royalty-free sound libraries
- Synthesize programmatically (pydub tone generation)

This is a prerequisite for tts-9lr (vibe-driven chime selection), which layers mood modulation on top of the per-signal base chimes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Adopt CLI standard: complete product surface on CLI
description:
Adopt punt-kit CLI standard: the CLI is the complete product. Every MCP tool and slash command has a corresponding CLI command. The CLI is the source of truth; MCP tools and slash commands project it.

## Core principle (from standard)

"A user who never opens Claude Code can use every feature."

Currently vox is inverted — the MCP server is the product and the CLI is secondary. This bead inverts that dependency: implement in CLI first, project to MCP/plugin.

## Current state (post vox-k0o)

CLI commands: `synthesize`, `synthesize-batch`, `synthesize-pair`, `synthesize-pair-batch`, `doctor`, `install`, `uninstall`, `install-desktop`, `play`, `mcp`

MCP tools: `speak`, `chorus`, `duet`, `ensemble`, `set_config`, `list_voices`

Slash commands: `/say`, `/recap`, `/notify`, `/speak`, `/vibe`, `/voice`

### Name mapping (important — names differ across surfaces)

| CLI (proposed) | MCP tool | Slash command | Notes |
|----------------|----------|---------------|-------|
| `say` | `speak` | `/say`, `/recap` | MCP is `speak`, slash is `/say`. Different names. |
| `notify` | `set_config` | `/notify` | Config mutation, not its own MCP tool |
| `speak` | `set_config` | `/speak` | **Name collision**: slash `/speak` toggles voice-vs-chime; MCP `speak` synthesizes audio |
| `vibe` | `set_config` | `/vibe` | Config mutation |
| `voice` | `set_config` + `list_voices` | `/voice` | `/voice` (no arg) calls `list_voices` for browsing; `/voice <name>` calls `set_config` |
| `chorus` | `chorus` | — | No slash command exists |
| `duet` | `duet` | — | No slash command exists |
| `ensemble` | `ensemble` | — | No slash command exists |

## Product commands needed (Layer 1)

### New commands — projecting existing MCP tools to CLI

- [ ] `say <text>` — synthesize + auto-play + ephemeral cleanup. Maps to MCP `speak` tool. This is NOT a rename of `synthesize` — `synthesize` writes a persistent file, `say` plays and discards.
- [ ] `chorus <texts...>` — multi-voice synthesis. Maps to MCP `chorus` tool.
- [ ] `duet <text1> <text2>` — paired bilingual synthesis. Maps to MCP `duet` tool.
- [ ] `ensemble <manifest>` — multi-voice batch from JSON manifest. Maps to MCP `ensemble` tool.

### New commands — projecting existing slash commands to CLI

These slash commands call `set_config` MCP tool. CLI equivalents read/write `.vox/config.md` directly (no MCP dependency).

- [ ] `notify y|c|n` — toggle task notifications (maps to `/notify`)
- [ ] `speak y|n` — toggle voice vs chime delivery (maps to `/speak`)
- [ ] `vibe <mood>|auto|off` — set session mood (maps to `/vibe`)
- [ ] `voice [on|off|status|<name>|clear]` — voice mode and session voice (maps to `/voice`). With no arg, lists available voices (subsumes the `list_voices` MCP tool — no separate `voices` command needed).

## Admin commands needed (Layer 2)

- [ ] `version` — output: `vox <semver>` (typer supports `--version` callback)
- [ ] `status` — current provider, voice, vibe, config state

## Existing commands that stay

- `synthesize` — persistent file output (not replaced by `say`)
- `synthesize-batch`, `synthesize-pair`, `synthesize-pair-batch` — batch variants
- `doctor`, `install`, `play`, `mcp` — keep as-is

## Existing commands to retire

- [ ] `install-desktop` — fold into `install` (subcommand or flag)
- [ ] `uninstall` — fold into `install --uninstall` or keep if cleaner

## README updates

The README CLI section is stale post vox-k0o:
- [ ] `vox serve` → `vox mcp`
- [ ] Add new product commands once implemented
- [ ] Document `say` vs `synthesize` distinction

## Projection alignment

After CLI commands exist, verify:
- MCP tools and CLI commands call the same core functions
- Slash commands instruct Claude to call the corresponding MCP tool
- No capability exists in plugin that CLI cannot do
- No capability exists in CLI that plugin cannot access

Depends on vox-k0o (completed — PR #59 merged).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #60 (mic API redesign)

title: elevenlabs SDK pydantic v1 warning on Python 3.14
description:
When running vox via uv tool install on Python 3.14, elevenlabs SDK emits:

```
elevenlabs/core/pydantic_utilities.py:13: UserWarning: Core Pydantic V1 functionality isn't compatible with Python 3.14 or greater.
  from pydantic.v1.datetime_parse import parse_date as parse_date
```

This is upstream in the elevenlabs Python SDK — they import pydantic v1 compat layer which is incompatible with 3.14. Not actionable on our side until elevenlabs ships a fix. Track to monitor and suppress the warning if it becomes user-facing noise.

Seen in: /Users/jfreeman/.local/share/uv/tools/punt-vox/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elevenlabs/core/pydantic_utilities.py:13
close_reason: Suppressed via warnings.filterwarnings in providers/__init__.py and pytest filterwarnings in pyproject.toml (PR #71)

title: Root-cause flaky watcher notification consumer tests on CI
description: test_throttles_same_signal and test_different_signals_not_throttled in test_watcher.py::TestNotificationConsumer fail on CI (GitHub Actions Ubuntu runner) but pass locally (macOS). _announce_voice mock call_count is 0 when expected 1 or 2. Skipped on CI with pytest.mark.skipif for now. Need to root-cause why the mock patch doesn't intercept on Linux/CI — possibly import path difference, threading issue, or config reading behavior difference.

title: Add /voice slash command to punt-tts plugin
description: The Claude Code plugin shell exists (plugin.json, MCP server entry) but has no custom slash commands. Add /voice on|off command that toggles voice mode via .claude/tts.local.md config. When voice is on, a PostToolUse hook auto-speaks synthesis results.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #1: session-start.sh with dev/prod namespace, voice.md + voice-dev.md commands

title: Rename MCP tools to fun, on-brand names
description: Rename the four MCP tools from clinical names to fun, voice/audio-themed names. Current names are technical and appear frequently in the UI: synthesize, synthesize_batch, synthesize_pair, synthesize_pair_batch. Follow the dungeon plugin pattern where tools were renamed from generic (load, save, delete) to thematic (recall, inscribe, obliterate). Naming should evoke the voice/audio domain — think about what a sound engineer, DJ, or voice actor would call these operations. Must update: server.py tool decorators, suppress-output.sh hook (tool name matching), tests, CLAUDE.md architecture table. Must coordinate with suppress-output.sh since it matches on tool_name.
close_reason: Merged PR #4 — renamed tools: speak, chorus, duet, ensemble

title: Remove debug vibe_mode output from stop hook block reason
description: The notify.sh stop hook includes 'vibe_mode: auto' in its block reason output, which leaks into the user-visible hook error display. The block reason should only contain the ♪ phrase. The vibe_mode data needs to be passed to the LLM some other way (e.g. structured metadata) or parsed out before display. Currently shows: 'Stop hook error: ♪ Voicing my closing remarks...\nvibe_mode: auto' — the second line is debug data that shouldn't be visible.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #26 — vibe data removed from block reason, instructions updated to read .tts/config.md

title: Phase 7: Downstream coordination
description:
Update all projects that depend on punt-tts.

- langlearn-tts: update dependency from punt-tts to punt-vox, update imports
- Parent workspace CLAUDE.md: project map, relationships, PyPI table
- biff plugin references (if any)
- Any other punt-labs projects referencing punt-tts
close_reason: Updated langlearn-tts (PR #36), parent CLAUDE.md, punt-kit standards (PR #25), marketplace (PR #14).

title: Phase 2: Rename config paths and env vars
description:
Update all runtime paths and environment variables.

- .tts/ ephemeral dir → .vox/
- .punt-tts/ log dir → .punt-vox/
- ~/tts-output/ default dir → ~/vox-output/
- TTS_OUTPUT_DIR env var → VOX_OUTPUT_DIR
- Update src/punt_vox/output.py, ephemeral.py, logging_config.py, server.py
- Update corresponding tests (test_output.py, test_ephemeral.py, etc.)
- Update .gitignore

Decision needed: support TTS_OUTPUT_DIR as fallback or clean break?
close_reason: Merged PR #51 — renamed TTS_OUTPUT_DIR→VOX_OUTPUT_DIR, ~/tts-output→~/vox-output, .tts/→.vox/, ~/.punt-tts/→~/.punt-vox/

title: Fix hooks to resolve .vox/config.md from git toplevel in worktrees
description: state.sh uses TTS_STATE_FILE=".vox/config.md" (relative to cwd). In a worktree, cwd is the worktree root where .vox/ doesn't exist (not in git). Hooks silently fail — read_notify returns 'n', all notifications disabled. Fix: resolve path via realpath "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)/.." in state.sh. This returns the main repo root from both worktrees and the main repo.
close_reason: Fixed in this PR — state.sh now resolves config via git-common-dir

title: install.sh does not upgrade already-installed plugin to current version
description: After releasing v0.6.0, the installed plugin remained at v0.4.0. Running install.sh (via install-all.sh) updated the PyPI package and ran `claude plugin marketplace update punt-labs`, but did not upgrade the already-installed plugin. Had to manually run `claude plugin install tts --marketplace punt-labs` to get v0.6.0. The install.sh should detect when the installed plugin version is behind the marketplace version and reinstall it.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #28 — install.sh now detects existing plugin and runs claude plugin update instead of no-op install

title: Architecture B: log-tailing MCP server for /notify c continuous mode
description:
Session event watcher: background thread in the MCP server that tails the active session JSONL. Foundational infrastructure for two consumers:

1. **Continuous notifications** (notify=c): announces milestones via speech — no model involvement, no extra tokens. Detects git commits, test completions, new files, subagent completions by pattern matching.

2. **Auto-vibe signals** (tts-5eo): feeds richer context to mood detection — model frustration patterns, user tone, error density over time, task arcs. Far richer than the current PostToolUse-only signal window.

Key design decisions:
1. Session discovery: find active JSONL by most-recently-modified in project dir
2. Throttle: minimum N seconds between announcements
3. Consumer architecture: single watcher thread, multiple consumers (notifications + mood scoring)
4. Config: reads .tts/config.md — notifications only active when notify=c, mood scoring only when vibe_mode=auto
5. Audio collision: must coordinate with stop hook and model-driven speak
6. Synthesis: uses existing provider pipeline
7. Respects speak=n chime mode

Complementary to tts-1p3 (screen reader mode). Screen reader fires synchronously after tool calls via hooks. The watcher fires asynchronously during long-running operations when the model is busy and hooks can't fire.

Current state: notify=c is accepted but behaves identically to notify=y. This bead builds the watcher and the notification consumer.
close_reason: Closed

title: Auto-vibe: detect session mood from hook signals
description:
Auto-vibe: detect session mood from transcript analysis and hook signals.

Two signal sources:
1. **JSONL watcher** (from tts-51v): tails session transcript for rich context — model frustration patterns (repeated retries), user tone (terse vs enthusiastic), error density over time, task arcs (debugging → breakthrough → cleanup). Primary signal source.
2. **PostToolUse hooks** (existing signal.sh): fast point-in-time signals from Bash exit codes — test_pass, lint_fail, git_push_ok, etc. Supplements the watcher.

Mood scoring combines both sources and writes vibe_tags to config. Stop hook and continuous notifications use these tags for voice styling.

Config: vibe_mode=auto enables transcript-based detection. vibe_mode=manual uses /vibe hint (current behavior). vibe_mode=off disables all mood styling. Manual /vibe always overrides auto.

Depends on tts-51v for the JSONL watcher infrastructure.

title: Phase 6: First release as punt-vox on PyPI
description:
Publish punt-vox to PyPI for the first time.

- Follow standard release workflow (CLAUDE.md 12-step)
- Tag and push to trigger GH Actions
- Verify: uv tool install punt-vox && vox doctor
- Update marketplace.json with new package name and tag ref
- This is the point of no return for the public name change
close_reason: Released v0.10.0 and v0.10.1 as punt-vox on PyPI. Marketplace updated.

title: Two-channel display output for MCP tools
description: Implement the two-channel display pattern (punt-kit/patterns/two-channel-display.md) for TTS MCP tool results. Current suppress-output.sh is functional but bland — 'Speaking: hello world...', 'Synthesized 3 items'. Follow biff's approach: unicode-formatted panel summaries with personality, and structured additionalContext for the model. Panel output should be compact, expressive, and max 80 columns. Consider emoji or unicode glyphs for visual identity (biff uses ▶). additionalContext should carry the full JSON result so the model can reason about paths, voices, languages without cluttering the panel.
close_reason: Merged PR #5 — two-channel display with ♪ prefix and voice/provider context

title: Fix double vibe tag prepend in speak tool output
description: When calling speak with vibe tags in the text (e.g. '[calm] Filed tts-e59...'), the result shows the tag doubled: '[calm] [calm] Filed tts-e59...'. The server or provider layer is prepending vibe_tags from config.md onto the text, but the caller is also including them. Either the server should not auto-prepend when tags are already present in the text, or the stop hook protocol should instruct the LLM not to include tags in the text (since set_config already sets them and the server prepends them).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #27 — _apply_vibe skips prepend when text already starts with a tag

title: fix: permission notification always uses matilda instead of session voice
close_reason: Fixed in ba87f3c — notify-permission.sh now reads session voice from config

title: Add roundtable tool for multi-voice sequence synthesis
description: Add a new MCP tool (roundtable) that accepts an array of {text, voice} objects and synthesizes them into a single merged audio file with configurable pause between segments. This enables multi-persona conversations (e.g. prfaq meeting-listen) to be voiced in 1 call per scene instead of N calls per line. Current batch tools (chorus, duet, ensemble) don't support arbitrary voice-per-segment sequences. Interface sketch: roundtable(segments=[{text, voice, vibe_tags?}, ...], pause_ms=500, merge=true, ephemeral=true). Depends on existing chorus merge infrastructure for MP3 stitching.

title: Audio queue: play utterances sequentially instead of kill-previous
description:
The kill-previous fix (267c700) prevents overlapping audio but discards the interrupted utterance. All three playback paths (MCP _play_audio, stop hook chime, notification hook chime/voice) should enqueue audio instead, so every utterance plays to completion in FIFO order.

Possible approaches:
- A lightweight daemon/socket that accepts file paths and plays them sequentially
- A named pipe or Unix domain socket that all three paths write to
- A lock file with a wait loop (simplest but blocks the caller)

The queue must work across Python (server.py) and bash (hooks) and across independent Claude Code sessions.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.4.0 via flock-based playback serialization (playback.py). enqueue() + play_audio() provide FIFO ordering across Python and bash callers.

title: /notify command: toggle task notifications y|c|n
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 5: Update CI/CD and release infrastructure
description:
Update build and release tooling.

- .github/workflows/release.yml: package name, entry point references
- install.sh: package name references
- scripts/*.sh: any punt-tts references
- Verify uv build && uvx twine check dist/* passes
- Test the full release workflow dry-run
close_reason: Merged PR #52 — release.yml installs punt-vox/vox, install.sh uses punt-vox package with vox binary

title: Short commands (/recap, /say) not available — only namespaced /tts:recap works
close_reason: Fixed by uninstall/reinstall after v0.3.4 plugin name fix

title: Design command for notify-only mode (notify: y without voice)
description: Currently /unmute sets notify: c (continuous = voice + notifications) and /vox on sets notify: y (chime-only notifications). But there is no command to go from continuous mode back to chime-only mode without fully disabling notifications. Design a command or argument (e.g. /vox chime, or /mute setting notify: y) that sets notify: y while leaving speak: n. This gives users a middle ground: chimes on task completion without spoken summaries.

title: Add speech-to-text (STT) transcription backend
description: Add transcription capability (e.g. Whisper, AWS Transcribe) as a new backend alongside existing synthesis providers. Enables audio understanding workflows — e.g. transcribing ephemeral MP3s for content analysis. This is the 'listening' half of Vox working in both directions.

title: Vibe-driven chime notifications (non-speak mode)
description:
When speak=n, all notifications currently play the same generic chime_done.mp3 or chime_prompt.mp3 regardless of session mood. This feature makes chime-only notifications vibe-aware.

Current state:
- assets/chime_done.mp3 — single task-complete chime
- assets/chime_prompt.mp3 — single permission-prompt chime
- Stop hook (notify.sh) plays chime_done.mp3 when speak=n
- Notification hook (notify-permission.sh) plays chime_prompt.mp3
- Watcher (_announce_chime) plays chime_done.mp3 for all signals

Desired behavior:
- Chime selection varies based on current vibe_tags/vibe mood from .tts/config.md
- Different tones for different moods (warm/happy → gentle major-key chime, focused → crisp minimal tone, tense → alert tone, etc.)
- Different tones for different signal types in watcher mode (tests-pass vs tests-fail vs git-push-ok)
- Could use pre-generated chime variants (asset library) or dynamically generate short clips via ElevenLabs Music API (depends on tts-0qi)

Design considerations:
- Pre-generated assets: fast, no API cost, finite palette — good for v1
- Dynamic generation: infinite variety, matches vibe exactly, but adds latency and API cost
- Hybrid: small asset library for common moods, fallback to generation for rare combinations
- Chime selection logic lives in playback path (hooks and watcher), reads vibe from config
- Signal-type differentiation: success signals get positive tones, failure signals get alert tones, independent of vibe
close_reason: Implemented in PR #58

title: Phase 3: Rename plugin and hooks
description:
Update Claude Code plugin identity and hook references.

- plugin.json: name tts → vox (and tts-dev → vox-dev)
- hooks/*.sh: update .tts/ references to .vox/
- commands/*.md: update .tts/ and tool name references
- hooks/state.sh: update path references
- scripts/release-plugin.sh and restore-dev-plugin.sh: update swap logic
- Update .claude/settings.json MCP tool auto-allow patterns
close_reason: Closed

title: Install should update plugin when already installed
description: When a user already has the tts plugin installed, running install.sh or tts install only checks claude plugin install which says 'already installed' and moves on. It never calls claude plugin update tts@punt-labs, so users stay stuck on their old version. The installer should: (1) try install, (2) if already installed, run update.
close_reason: PR #14 merged: installer now calls claude plugin update when already installed, plus --scope user fix from Bugbot review

title: Rename MCP server key from tts to vox
description: Rename the MCP server key in plugin.json from 'tts' to 'vox' so the display reads plugin:tts:vox instead of plugin:tts:tts. Breaking change: all allowed-tools references in commands change from mcp__plugin_tts_tts__* to mcp__plugin_tts_vox__*. Also update suppress-output.sh hook matcher, session-start.sh auto-allow patterns, and release-plugin.sh sed patterns.
close_reason: Closed

title: Notification hook: permission-prompt and idle-prompt audio alerts
close_reason: Closed

title: macOS say fallback provider (Fred voice) when no API keys configured
description: Add a macOS `say` command fallback provider that activates when no API keys (ElevenLabs, OpenAI, Polly) are configured. Uses the Fred voice — it's so bad it's good, and clearly signals 'configure a real provider.' Implementation: new SayProvider in providers/say.py implementing TTSProvider protocol. Provider registry auto-detects it as last resort. Outputs AIFF via `say -o`, converts to MP3 via afconvert or ffmpeg. Zero config, zero cost, fully offline.
close_reason: PR #30 — feat/say-provider branch

title: Support dev-only and dev+prod dual plugin environments
description:
Currently running `claude --plugin-dir .` loads the dev plugin (tts-dev) alongside the installed prod plugin (tts). This dual-env causes confusing behavior — e.g. dev MCP tool calls can route through the prod `tts serve` binary, which runs older code without recent fixes (observed: _apply_vibe double-tag prepend because prod lacks PR #27 fix).

Two environments to support:
1. **Dev-only**: only the working tree plugin is active, using the editable install binary. No prod plugin interference.
2. **Dev+prod dual**: both namespaces active simultaneously (current behavior). Useful for comparing prod vs dev behavior.

Needs investigation into how to cleanly isolate the dev environment when desired — potentially a flag, env var, or workflow change. The key problem is that `tts serve` in plugin.json resolves to whichever `tts` binary is on PATH, which may be the prod install rather than the editable install.

title: Add Claude Code plugin shell: /voice, hooks, config
description: Add plugin integration points following quarry pattern. plugin.json declaring MCP server. SessionStart hook (deploy commands, auto-allow permissions). PostToolUse suppress-output hook (hide JSON blobs). /voice on|off command. .claude/tts.local.md config (voice name, auto-speak triggers). Dev/prod namespace isolation (tts-dev / tts).
close_reason: Plugin shell shipped in v0.1.0 (phase 4): plugin.json, hooks, /voice, /say

title: Improve command help strings with exact input examples
description: Add argument-hint frontmatter to all tts commands (pattern from biff). Shows autocomplete-style hints in the slash command picker. Examples: /notify → 'y | c | n', /speak → 'y | n', /say → '<text>', /voice → 'on | off | status', /recap → (none, no args).
close_reason: Added argument-hint frontmatter to notify, speak, say, voice commands

title: Phase 8: GitHub repo rename and PyPI deprecation
description:
Final cleanup after everything else is stable.

- Rename GitHub repo punt-labs/tts → punt-labs/vox (GitHub preserves redirects)
- Publish final punt-tts version on PyPI that prints deprecation notice pointing to punt-vox
- Update any remaining external links or references
- Verify all redirects work
close_reason: Repo renamed to punt-labs/vox. All file references updated, prfaq.pdf rebuilt, marketplace catalog and parent CLAUDE.md updated.

title: Audio collision: prevent overlapping TTS utterances
description: Two problems: (1) Cross-session collision — multiple Claude Code sessions with TTS can speak simultaneously, talking over each other. Needs a shared lock or coordination layer. (2) Same-session double-fire — occasionally two utterances play from the same session for a single event. Root cause unknown — could be stop hook + notification hook both triggering, or a race in the loop guard. Need to reproduce and diagnose both paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: 'said the piece' → 'said her piece' in speak hook output
description:
The PostToolUse hook for speak outputs 'matilda said the piece' which is not idiomatic English. The correct phrase is 'said her piece' (the idiom is 'to say one's piece').

Need to make the possessive pronoun match the voice name/gender, or use a gender-neutral form like 'had her say' or 'said their piece'.

Location: likely in hooks/suppress-output.sh where MCP tool output is formatted for the UI panel.
close_reason: Fixed in 7a0f876 on fix/said-her-piece

title: Add speech-to-text (STT) and rename to Vox
description: Vox works in both directions: TTS (existing) and STT (new). Rename punt-tts to punt-vox across PyPI, CLI entry point, plugin name, and marketplace catalog. Add transcription capability (e.g. Whisper) as a new backend alongside existing ElevenLabs/Polly/OpenAI synthesis. This enables audio understanding workflows — e.g. transcribing ephemeral MP3s for content analysis.
close_reason: Split: rename already tracked by tts-onr epic; creating separate STT bead

title: Add personality to chime-only confirmation message
description: When the user runs /speak n to silence TTS, the confirmation message is currently 'Chime-only notifications. No words.' This is flat and robotic. Replace with a rotating set of short, personality-rich confirmations that convey the same sentiment — e.g. 'OK, I'll zip it.', 'Going silent.', 'Lips sealed.', 'Muting myself.', 'Say no more.' The response should freelance from a pool rather than always returning the same string.

title: Move config from global ~/.claude/tts.local.md to per-project .tts/config.md
description: tts.local.md is at ~/.claude/ (global), so /notify, /speak, /voice affect ALL sessions across all projects. Should be per-project like biff's .biff/ directory. Move config to .tts/config.md in the repo root. The .tts/ directory already exists for ephemeral audio — add config.md alongside it. Update: state.sh (hooks reader), all commands that read/write config, session-start.sh, and .gitignore guidance.
close_reason: PR #11 merged — config moved to per-project .tts/config.md

title: Config path resolution assumes git for standalone pip users
description: resolve_config_path() shells out to git rev-parse on every CLI config command (notify, speak, voice, vibe, status). For Claude Code this is correct — it finds the git root and shares config across worktrees. But for standalone pip install users (no git repo, possibly no git binary), it spawns a wasted subprocess that always fails before falling back to CWD-relative DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH. Options: (1) walk up dirs looking for .vox/ without git, (2) check for git cheaply before shelling out, (3) accept the overhead since the fallback works. The Claude Code case is fully correct today — this is only about the standalone CLI experience.

title: Streaming TTS: use ElevenLabs stream API to reduce time-to-first-audio
description: Replace batch text_to_speech.convert() with text_to_speech.stream() for the speak MCP tool. The text is already known upfront so this is HTTP streaming (not WebSocket), but audio chunks start playing before full synthesis completes. Reduces perceived latency from ~1-2s to ~75ms with Flash v2.5 model. Does not change the API surface — callers still get an MP3 file back. The stream() method returns Iterator[bytes] which we write to disk as chunks arrive, then play. Optional: pipe chunks directly to afplay via stdin for true streaming playback (no temp file). This is a latency optimization, not a feature change.
close_reason: Released in v0.3.6

title: Investigate missing voice name in speak tool suppress-output display
description: The suppress-output.sh PostToolUse hook formats MCP tool results for the UI panel. When speak completes, it shows '♪ the voice has spoken' but should include the voice name (e.g. '♪ matilda said the piece'). The raw result JSON includes the voice field — the hook's formatting logic may be dropping it or using a fallback string. Compare with other tool outputs that do show voice names correctly.
close_reason: Fixed in 505ca71 — added result wrapper unwrapping to suppress-output.sh jq extraction

title: Bug: vibe tags prepended to text for non-ElevenLabs providers
description:
_apply_vibe() in server.py prepends expressive tags like [tired] [calm] to text unconditionally. ElevenLabs eleven_v3 interprets these as performance cues, but Polly and OpenAI speak them as literal words.

Fix: gate _apply_vibe() on the active provider being ElevenLabs, or strip bracket tags before sending to non-ElevenLabs providers.

Location: src/punt_tts/server.py:136-145 (_apply_vibe) and call sites at lines 321, 391.
close_reason: PR #39 merged — _apply_vibe gated on provider.supports_expressive_tags

title: Stop hook: task-completion spoken notification
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 4: Update documentation
description:
Update all docs to reflect the new name.

- CLAUDE.md: all references to punt-tts, tts, punt_tts, paths
- README.md: package name, CLI examples, install instructions
- DESIGN.md: architecture references
- CHANGELOG.md: add rename entry under Unreleased
- prfaq.tex: punt-tts references (~60 occurrences)
close_reason: Closed

title: Ghost user messages from session continuation
description: During session restoration from context compaction, fake 'user' messages appear in the conversation due to API turn-taking requirements (strict user/assistant alternation). These phantom messages have triggered Claude to take action on instructions the user never gave (e.g. merging PRs, cutting builds). Observed multiple times. Appears related to TTS plugin — possibly the stop hook's block/continue pattern creates extra turns that get misattributed during compaction recovery. Need to investigate: (1) whether the stop hook continuation pattern contributes to turn misalignment, (2) whether compaction summaries are being slotted into user turns, (3) potential mitigations (e.g. always confirming destructive actions after session restore).

title: Session mood via ElevenLabs audio tags
description:
New /mood command that sets an ElevenLabs eleven_v3 audio tag for the session. All TTS utterances embed the tag (e.g. [dramatic tone], [whisper], [excited], [tired]). Store in tts.local.md, inject into speak tool calls. Fun/viral feature — people sharing clips of their AI whispering code reviews or dramatically announcing releases. Only works with ElevenLabs eleven_v3 model.

Stretch: time-aware auto-mood. Session-start hook checks the clock and sets a default mood — normal during the day, [tired] after 10pm, [whisper] after midnight. The voice naturally adapts to late-night coding sessions without any user action.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #23: /vibe command with two-field model (vibe + vibe_tags), Claude-as-voice-director interpretation, ElevenLabs expressive tags. Auto-vibe tracked separately in tts-5eo.

title: Chime audio: bundled MP3 tones for /speak n mode
close_reason: Closed

title: Adopt CLI hook dispatcher pattern (vox hook <event>)
description: Migrate hook business logic from bash (state.sh, notify.sh, signal.sh, suppress-output.sh, notify-permission.sh) to Python hooks.py module with testable pure functions. Shell scripts become thin gates that check preconditions and delegate to 'vox hook <event>'. Per punt-kit/standards/cli.md Layer 3.
close_reason: All hook scripts are thin dispatchers delegating to vox hook <event>. Business logic in hooks.py with full test coverage.

title: Stop hook: pass vibe config in block reason instead of requiring Read
description: The MCP server description tells Claude to 'use the Read tool to read .vox/config.md' on every stop hook continuation to get vibe_mode, vibe, and vibe_signals. This is wasteful — one extra tool call per task completion. Fix: have notify.sh include the vibe fields in the block reason JSON (it already reads config.md to check notify status), then update the server description to use the fields from the block reason instead of reading the file. Alternative: use 'vox status --json' via Bash instead of raw file read — still one tool call but goes through the CLI.
close_reason: Merged PR #74: MCP call paths for all commands, vibe context in stop hook

title: Add hooks to punt-tts Claude Code plugin
description: The plugin.json exists but no hooks are implemented. Needs: SessionStart hook (deploy commands, auto-allow MCP permissions), PostToolUse hook (suppress raw JSON output from synthesis tools, auto-play audio). Follow quarry/biff plugin patterns.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #1: session-start.sh with dev/prod namespace, voice.md + voice-dev.md commands

title: Adopt CLI standard: click→typer migration
description:
Bring vox CLI into compliance with punt-kit/standards/cli.md. Structural migration — framework, entry point, hook architecture, global flags.

## Current state
- Framework: click (FAIL — standard requires typer)
- Entry point: punt_vox.cli:main (FAIL — should be punt_vox.__main__:app)
- No __main__.py exists
- Separate vox-server script entry in pyproject.toml (should be `vox mcp`)
- `serve` command does stdio transport (standard: `mcp` for stdio, `serve` for HTTP)
- doctor: exists (PASS)
- install: exists (PASS)
- --json: exists (PASS)
- --verbose: exists (PASS)
- --quiet: missing (FAIL)
- No shell completion support

## Gaps to fix
- [ ] Migrate click → typer
- [ ] Disable rich panels for plain text help
- [ ] Create __main__.py with `app = typer.Typer()`
- [ ] Update pyproject.toml entry point to `punt_vox.__main__:app`
- [ ] Remove vox-server script entry, rename `serve` → `mcp` (stdio transport)
- [ ] Add `--quiet`/`-q` global flag (mutually exclusive with `--verbose`)
- [ ] Move `--provider` and `--model` from global to subcommand options
- [ ] Add shell completion via typer `--install-completion` / `--show-completion`

## Hook architecture migration
- [ ] Create `src/punt_vox/hooks.py` with pure handler functions (testable, no I/O)
- [ ] Add `vox hook <event>` hidden CLI subcommand as dispatcher
- [ ] Refactor 7 shell scripts to thin gates: check config, delegate to `vox hook <event>`
- [ ] Shell gates: fail-open on observation (PostToolUse, SessionStart), fail-closed on mutation

## Command architecture decision
- [ ] Choose between Direct Delegation and Humble Object Commands pattern
- [ ] If Humble Object: create `src/punt_vox/commands/` with `CommandResult` dataclass and `_run()` adapter
- [ ] Vox has commands that orchestrate multiple core calls (vibe + config + synthesis + playback), suggesting Humble Object pattern

Part of org-level epic punt-kit-ozm.
close_reason: PR #59 merged: Click→Typer migration complete

title: Add ephemeral output mode for Claude Code sessions
description: Add ephemeral parameter to synthesize MCP tools. When ephemeral=true: write to .tts/ in project root (gitignored), delete previous files before each new synthesis, delete last file on session end (SessionEnd hook). When ephemeral=false (default): write to ~/tts-output/, persist files. Supports both voice-plugin use (ephemeral) and langlearn use (persistent).
close_reason: Ephemeral output mode shipped in v0.1.0 (phase 3)

title: Create punt-tts: extract general-purpose TTS from langlearn-tts
description: Fork langlearn-tts into punt-labs/tts. Strip langlearn-types dependency. Define own types locally (SynthesisRequest, SynthesisResult, TTSProvider). Add ephemeral parameter to synthesize (write to .tts/, auto-delete after playback). Default output dir ~/tts-output/. CLI command: tts. PyPI: punt-tts. All 3 providers (ElevenLabs, Polly, OpenAI), all 4 MCP tools (synthesize, batch, pair, pair-batch), doctor, install. Quality gates: ruff, mypy, pyright, pytest.
close_reason: v0.1.0 released to PyPI — source, tests, CI, release pipeline all complete

title: Cosmetic: marketplace install.sh prints stale punt-labs/tts example URLs
description: The claude-plugins repo install.sh prints example curl URLs that still reference punt-labs/tts/main/install.sh instead of punt-labs/vox. These are informational only (nobody uses them — install-all.sh uses SHA-pinned URLs), but they're confusing. Fix is in claude-plugins/install.sh, not in vox.

title: Auto-add ElevenLabs community voices to user library via API
description: When a voice name resolves to a community/shared voice that isn't in the user's library, auto-add it via the ElevenLabs API (POST /v1/shared-voices/add or equivalent). Currently, VoiceNotFoundError is thrown with no fallback. Discovered during prfaq v2.0 integration — prfaq meeting personas use community voices (yu, nila) that require library membership. Downstream consumers (prfaq meeting-listen) work around this with built-in fallback voices, but the real fix is transparent auto-add in TTS.

title: MCP tool for config writes instead of file-tool edits
description: Commands like /vibe, /voice, /notify, /speak all modify .tts/config.md using Claude's Read/Write/Edit file tools. This works but is inelegant — the user sees file operations in the panel for what should be a simple config toggle. Add an MCP tool (e.g. 'config' or 'set') that writes config fields server-side. Commands call the MCP tool instead of editing files directly. Benefits: cleaner panel output (no file-write noise), atomic config updates, single source of truth for config parsing (server already reads config for vibe_tags). The tool would accept field/value pairs and update .tts/config.md internally.
close_reason: set_config MCP tool implemented and in production use. All commands (/vibe, /voice, /notify, /speak) use it instead of file-tool edits.

title: PreCompact hook: playful 'be right back' speech
description:
Add a PreCompact hook that triggers a short spoken message when context compaction begins. The message should be playful and varied — like the AI is stepping away briefly:

- "Grabbing a snack, be right back"
- "Quick bathroom break, one sec"
- "Let me clear my head for a moment"
- "Stretching my legs, back in a flash"
- "Refilling my coffee, hold tight"

## Implementation

- Hook on `PreCompact` event (both `manual` and `auto` matchers)
- Use `speak` with `ephemeral=true` 
- Randomly select from a pool of playful BRB messages
- Should be quick and non-blocking (compaction shouldn't wait)
- Respect notify/speak config — only fire if notifications are on
- Use current session voice and vibe

## Why

Compaction is a jarring moment — the AI goes silent, context resets, and the user waits. A quick spoken quip humanizes the transition and signals "I'm still here, just reorganizing."

title: Rename punt-tts/tts to punt-vox/vox
description:
Rename the entire project from punt-tts/tts to punt-vox/vox.

## Scope

### PyPI & Package
- PyPI package: punt-tts → punt-vox
- CLI entry point: tts → vox
- MCP server entry point: tts-server → vox-server
- Python package: punt_tts → punt_vox
- src/punt_tts/ → src/punt_vox/

### Plugin
- Plugin name: tts → vox (plugin.json)
- Dev plugin name: tts-dev → vox-dev
- MCP server name in plugin: vox (already named vox)
- Hook references to tts paths
- Command files referencing tts

### Configuration & Paths
- TTS_OUTPUT_DIR env var → VOX_OUTPUT_DIR (or keep for compat?)
- ~/.punt-tts/ log dir → ~/.punt-vox/
- ~/tts-output/ default dir → ~/vox-output/
- .tts/ ephemeral dir → .vox/
- .tts/config.md → .vox/config.md

### Repository
- GitHub repo: punt-labs/tts → punt-labs/vox
- CLAUDE.md references
- Parent workspace CLAUDE.md references
- README, CHANGELOG

### Downstream
- langlearn-tts depends on punt-tts — needs coordinated update
- Parent CLAUDE.md project map
- Marketplace registration
- Any installed plugin references

## Notes
- Need to decide on deprecation strategy for punt-tts on PyPI
- GitHub repo rename preserves redirects
- MCP server is already named 'vox' internally
close_reason: All 8 phases complete. PyPI package is punt-vox, CLI is vox, plugin is vox, GitHub repo is punt-labs/vox. PyPI deprecation notice on punt-tts skipped (no users). Rename is done.

title: Submit punt-tts to Claude Code marketplace and Desktop extension directory
description: Build .mcpb Desktop Extension bundle. Submit to Claude Desktop extension directory. Submit plugin to Claude Code marketplace. Verify install flow: curl install.sh, tts doctor, /voice on.
close_reason: Marketplace installer shipped in v0.2.0. Plugin released to PyPI, tagged, GitHub release created.

title: Remove recap-dev and say-dev commands from prod plugin install
description: The production plugin install (via marketplace/tagged release) currently includes recap-dev.md and say-dev.md command files. These are dev-namespace commands meant only for local development testing (--plugin-dir .) and should be stripped by scripts/release-plugin.sh before tagging. Verify the release script removes *-dev.md commands and that the latest published release doesn't contain them.
close_reason: Not a bug: v0.6.0 prod install is clean (no dev commands). The recap-dev and say-dev commands appear only when running with --plugin-dir . (dev mode), which is by design. The release-plugin.sh script correctly strips *-dev.md commands before tagging.

title: bug: AskUserQuestion dialog does not render inside skill/command execution
description:
When AskUserQuestion is called during a /voice skill execution, the dialog never appears. The tool returns immediately with empty answers. AskUserQuestion works correctly when called outside skill context (tested and confirmed). Suppress-output hook only matches MCP tools so it shouldn't interfere, but the interaction hasn't been ruled out.

## Investigation plan
1. Test AskUserQuestion inside a command with and without the suppress-output hook active
2. Test AskUserQuestion as the terminal action of a skill (continuation style)

## Potential fix: continuation-passing style
Structure the command so list_voices + formatting happens first, then AskUserQuestion is the very last action (the skill 'exits with' the dialog). When the user answers, the model already has context and completes the set_config call. This works if the issue is mid-skill rendering, not terminal-action rendering.

title: Fix restore-dev-plugin.sh: fails when .claude/commands/ doesn't exist
description: restore-dev-plugin.sh exits 1 with 'error: pathspec .claude/commands/ did not match any file(s) known to git' when there are no dev command files. The script tries to git checkout commands that don't exist. Also release-plugin.sh exits 1 with 'No -dev commands found' warning even though the name swap succeeds. Both scripts need to handle the no-dev-commands case gracefully.
close_reason: Closed

title: Release CI: TestPyPI verification step fails after successful publish
description: The release workflow publishes to TestPyPI successfully, but the subsequent verification step (pip install from TestPyPI) reports failure. This has happened on multiple releases (v0.4.0, v0.5.0, v0.6.0 — all required manual workflow_dispatch reruns). Likely a race condition: TestPyPI index hasn't propagated the new version by the time the verify step runs. Investigate adding a retry/delay or switching to a polling check.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #29 — added --refresh to uv pip install to bust stale index cache between retry attempts

title: Adopt CLI global flag standard (--quiet, mutual exclusion)
description:
## Context

Audit against punt-kit/standards/cli.md §Global Flags found 2 gaps. Vox is mostly compliant — it already has `_emit()`, correct flag ordering for existing flags, and no argv hoisting. Two changes bring it to full compliance.

## What's correct

- **Flag ordering**: `--json` before `--verbose/-v` (src/punt_vox/__main__.py:178-186)
- **No argv hoisting**: Only sys.argv use is in the isolated playback subprocess (playback.py:102), not the main CLI
- **`_emit()` adapter**: Centralized at lines 57-64, used by most commands
- **no_args_is_help=True**: Functionally equivalent to invoke_without_command

## What needs to change

### 1. Add `--quiet/-q` flag
**File:** `src/punt_vox/__main__.py:97-100`
- Add `Quiet` type alias alongside existing `Verbose` type alias
- Add `quiet: Quiet = False` parameter to `_callback()` after `verbose`
- Store in module-level `_quiet_output` global (matches existing `_json_output` pattern)
- Wire into `_emit()`: suppress text output when quiet and not JSON mode

### 2. Add mutual exclusion validation
**File:** `src/punt_vox/__main__.py:178-186` (in `_callback()`)
- Add after line 186:
  ```python
  if verbose and quiet:
      raise typer.BadParameter("--verbose and --quiet are mutually exclusive.")
  ```

### Optional: consolidate inline JSON checks
`status_cmd()` (lines 560-574) and `doctor()` (lines 727-731) have inline `if _json_output:` checks instead of using `_emit()`. Not blocking, but inconsistent with the rest of the CLI.

## Reference implementations
- **quarry** `_emit()` pattern: `quarry/src/quarry/__main__.py:102-113`
- **biff** mutual exclusion: `biff/src/biff/__main__.py:115-116`
close_reason: Added --quiet/-q flag, mutual exclusion with --verbose, _emit() suppresses text in quiet mode.

title: PostToolUse hook matcher doesn't match standalone MCP tool names
description:
TTS plugin's PostToolUse suppress-output hook only fires when tts is loaded as a plugin, not as a standalone MCP server.

## Root Cause

The matcher in hooks.json is:
```
"matcher": "mcp__plugin_tts(-dev)?_vox__.*"
```

This matches plugin-namespaced tool names (`mcp__plugin_tts_vox__speak`, `mcp__plugin_tts-dev_vox__speak`) but NOT standalone MCP server names (`mcp__tts_vox__speak` or `mcp__vox__speak`).

Less likely to hit than quarry (tts is typically loaded as a plugin), but the same class of bug. If someone adds tts to .mcp.json as a standalone server, suppress-output won't fire.

## Fix

Update the matcher to handle both patterns:
```json
"matcher": "mcp__(plugin_tts(-dev)?_)?vox__.*"
```

This matches:
- `mcp__vox__*` (standalone)
- `mcp__plugin_tts_vox__*` (installed plugin)
- `mcp__plugin_tts-dev_vox__*` (dev plugin)

## Cross-repo

Same bug exists in quarry (quarry-fsx) and biff. Pattern fix is identical across all three.
close_reason: Closed

title: /recap command: on-demand spoken summary of last response
close_reason: Closed

title: Add vibe_tags parameter to speak tool
description:
The stop-hook flow currently requires two tool calls: set_config (to apply vibe tags and clear signals) then speak. This produces two ♪ lines in the panel for what should be one action.

Add an optional vibe_tags parameter to the speak and chorus MCP tools. When provided, the tool applies the tags and clears vibe_signals internally before synthesis — no separate set_config needed.

Before (2 calls, 2 panel lines):
  set_config({vibe_tags: "[warm] [satisfied]", vibe_signals: ""})
  → ♪ vibe shifted to [warm] [satisfied]
  speak(text="All done.", ephemeral=true)
  → ♪ matilda has spoken

After (1 call, 1 panel line):
  speak(text="All done.", ephemeral=true, vibe_tags="[warm] [satisfied]")
  → ♪ matilda has spoken
close_reason: Closed

title: Permission notification phrase variation
description: notify-permission.sh currently uses fixed phrases: 'Needs your approval.' (permission_prompt) and 'Waiting for your input.' (idle_prompt). Hearing the exact same phrase every time is annoying and robotic. Add a pool of ~5-8 natural variations per notification type and randomly select one. Examples for permission_prompt: 'Hey, quick approval needed.', 'Need a green light here.', 'Got a question for you.', 'Your call on this one.' The variations should sound human and conversational, not corporate. Use bash RANDOM or shuf for selection. Keep the pool in the script itself (no external config).
close_reason: Merged PR #3 — phrase pools with Bash 3.2-compatible pick_random

title: Playful panel output: personify the voice
description: Change suppress-output.sh panel lines from factual metadata to playful personification. speak: '♪ matilda has spoken' instead of '♪ spoken — matilda (elevenlabs)'. Drop provider from display. Apply same pattern to duet/chorus/ensemble. Add phrase pool for variety like the stop hook.
close_reason: Personified panel output with phrase pools in suppress-output.sh
